name: CaneyCloud
one_liner: Hotel operating system for Venezuela — posadas, boutique hotels, and hostels sell, operate, and collect payments from one place.
overview:
CaneyCloud is a hotel operating system built for Venezuelan hospitality — posadas, boutique hotels, hostels, glamping, and apartment-hotels. It brings reservations, WhatsApp sales, payments, and invoicing into a single platform, replacing the scattered spreadsheets, chat groups, and disconnected payment systems small operators run on today.

Beyond what the walkthrough below shows, the platform also covers channel sync against Booking.com, Airbnb, Expedia and direct sales, a point of sale for restaurant, minibar, tours and spa, operational inventory, and dynamic pricing driven by occupancy and demand.

article.heading: One screen to run the day
article.html: The day starts on the Today dashboard. Operations cards surface exactly what needs a decision right now — pending collections, arrivals, departures, guests in house, and anything overdue that requires immediate action. Below them, the business pulse tracks today's revenue, the month in progress, occupancy, new and cancelled bookings, and the live BCV exchange rate, because every Venezuelan hotel operates in dollars and bolívares at once. Today's reservations and the latest guest messages sit on the same screen, so nothing waits in another tab.

image.alt: CaneyCloud Today dashboard showing operations cards, business pulse metrics with the BCV rate, today's reservations, and guest messages
image.caption: The Today dashboard — operations cards, business pulse, and today's reservations in one view.

article.heading: A calendar that prices every night in two currencies
article.html: The reservation calendar is a drag-and-drop grid of rooms against nights, organized by zone and room type. Every cell shows that night's rate in USD and its bolívar equivalent at the current BCV rate, and reservations stretch across the grid as bars you can move and resize. Availability, blocks, and walk-ins are managed from the same surface, and because the calendar is synced against the OTA channels, a night sold anywhere is a night closed everywhere — no overbooking.

image.alt: CaneyCloud reservation calendar grid showing rooms by zone with nightly rates in USD and bolívares and reservation bars across dates
image.caption: The reservation calendar — nightly rates in USD and VES for every room, with reservations as draggable bars.

article.heading: WhatsApp is the front desk
article.html: Venezuelan hotels sell on WhatsApp, so CaneyCloud treats it as the front desk. The inbox unifies WhatsApp and Instagram conversations with guests and prospects, and an AI agent answers with the hotel's own context — availability, rates, policies — in the guest's language. The team watches everything: conversations are filtered by AI-handled, escalated, or human, and one click takes control of any thread. The agent sells and serves; the team stays in charge.

image.alt: CaneyCloud inbox showing WhatsApp and Instagram conversations with an AI agent replying to a guest in French under team oversight
image.caption: The inbox — the AI agent answers a returning guest in French while the team keeps oversight.

article.heading: Every guest, remembered
article.html: Every conversation and reservation feeds a built-in CRM. Each guest profile carries their last visit, visit count, loyalty points, verification status, and lifetime value, so the hotel knows exactly who is checking in and what they're worth to the business. Opening a guest pulls up their conversation history right beside the table — the context follows the person, not the channel they wrote on.

image.alt: CaneyCloud guests CRM table with visits, points, and lifetime value columns, and a guest conversation panel open alongside
image.caption: The guest CRM — visits, points, and lifetime value at a glance, with the guest's conversation one click away.

moat: Built Venezuela-first, where global PMS vendors don't reach: dual-currency operation (USD and VES) with SENIAT-compatible fiscal invoicing, channel sync designed for zero overbooking across OTAs and direct sales, and WhatsApp — the channel Venezuelan hotels actually sell on — treated as a first-class sales surface with AI assistance under team oversight.
business_model:
SaaS subscription for hospitality operators, on three tiers: Free with usage limits — no credit card, enough to get a small property running — Premium at $300/month, and Premium Plus at $500/month for larger teams and properties.

On top of subscriptions, CaneyCloud earns a 1% fee on every transaction handled through the platform — so revenue scales with the hotels' own sales, and the incentive stays aligned: the platform only earns more when its hotels sell more.

chart.title: First five years — simulated projection
chart.note: Simulated: paying properties start at 5 in month 1 and follow an S-curve to ~245 by year 5. Subscriptions = paying properties × $360/month blended (70% Premium at $300, 30% Premium Plus at $500). Transaction fees = 1% of each property's payment volume at the $80k/year posada average (≈$67/property/month). Total = subscriptions + fees.

specifics.Market: Posadas, boutique hotels, hostels, glamping — Venezuela
specifics.Product: PMS + channel manager + POS + WhatsApp AI agent

name: VamosaVenezuela
one_liner: A booking platform for the Venezuela you haven't seen — curated stays, dining, and experiences across every region of the country.
overview:
VamosaVenezuela is a travel guide and trip-planning platform for Venezuelan tourism. It curates places to stay, dining, and activities across eight regions — Los Roques, Mérida and the Andes, Canaima and Angel Falls, Morrocoy, Margarita, Gran Sabana, Caracas, Barquisimeto — plus landscape categories from beaches and islands to tepuis and jungle.

Beyond destination guides, the platform ships real planning tools: an interactive map, flight search, sample trips, and a custom itinerary builder. Every destination carries an editorial safety rating, so travelers know what to expect before they commit.
moat: Editorial curation plus a per-destination safety-rating system — a trust layer no generic travel site offers for Venezuela. The content depth across all eight regions, hidden gems included, is the kind of ground-truth coverage a competitor can't scrape together quickly.
business_model: Audience-first: the platform builds traffic through curated destination content, with flight search and stay/experience listings as the natural referral and booking surfaces to monetize as the audience grows.

specifics.Tools: Interactive map, flight search, itinerary builder

name: PIPA Database
one_liner: A centralized operating system for e-commerce brands to handle analytics, decision making, and advertising.
overview: PIPA Database is a centralized operating system for e-commerce brands: analytics, decision making, and advertising handled from one place. It is still incubating — "PIPA Database" is a working title, and the full write-up will land here as the build matures.
